title: `gawk` segfault with QEMU in `machine-os` 5.0, 5.1 and 5.2
body:
### Issue Description

I'm using containers to build OpenWrt firmware. Debian based containers are used and on a native Podman setup everything works as expected.

Within the last month something changed around `machine-os` so it won't work anymore on my development machine,  a macOS M1 device.

For whatever reason, `gawk` fails to run and break QEMU somehow:

```shell
buildbot@39365009ae35:~$ gawk --version
qemu-x86_64-static: QEMU internal SIGSEGV {code=MAPERR, addr=0x20}
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

The containers:
* `ghcr.io/openwrt/imagebuilder:x86-64-master`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start container `ghcr.io/openwrt/imagebuilder:x86-64-master`
2. Run `gawk --version`
3. See core dump


### Describe the results you received

`gawk` is not usable, breaking the build system required to create firmware.

### Describe the results you expected

I'd expect to create firmware.

### podman info output

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Known issue, I think. Podman stopped supporting QEMU and recommends Rosetta instead, afaik.

* https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22714

Supposed to be fixed with QEMU 9.0, so upgrading that fc40 package manually is an alternative...

https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/2168
Yes podman 5.1 uses rosetta by default (you need to recreate the machine I think)
Yea I tried rosetta, too. It fails... differently but I couldn't nail down the issue there just yet.

> Supposed to be fixed with QEMU 9.0, so upgrading that fc40 package manually is an alternative...

Ideas on how to do that with machine-os:5.x as a base? It seems to use a read-only filesystem

> Ideas on how to do that with machine-os:5.x as a base? It seems to use a read-only filesystem

Previously it was using CoreOS and you could use rpm-ostree, now I think you do a new image?

Something like

rpm-ostree install https://kojipkgs.fedoraproject.org//packages/qemu/9.0.0/1.fc41/aarch64/qemu-user-static-x86-9.0.0-1.fc41.aarch64.rpm

And then reboot

----

Using fc39 might be easier (i.e. downgrade)

* https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22714#discussioncomment-9443666

But maybe only for Podman v4.9, before ORAS

I'm not sure if the new "[Podman Appliance](https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os)" supports any such modifications, or how to do them.
